message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We compared the physiological differences between exercising wearing a TNT or a double-layer-cotton (DLC) facemask (FM) and not wearing a mask (NM). Sixteen volunteers underwent 4 sets (S) of 2 sequential bouts (B). B1 and B2 corresponded to light and moderate intensity cycling, respectively. FMs were used as follows: S1: NM; S2: TNT or DLC; S3: DLC or TNT; and S4: NM. Metabolic, pulmonary, and perceptual variables were collected. The main results are expressed as effect sizes and confidence intervals (ES [95%CI]) for TNT and DLC unless otherwise indicated. Compared to NM, FM increased the duty cycle (B1=1.11[0.58-1.61] and 1.53[0.81-2.18]; B2=1.27[0.63-1.84] and 1.93[0.97-2.68]) and decreased breath frequency (B1=0.59[0.23-0.94] and 1.43[0.79-2.07], B2=0.39[0.05-0.71] and 1.33[0.71-1.94]). Only B1 tidal volume increased (0.33[0.09-0.56] and 0.62[0.18-1.05]) enough to avoid a ventilation reduction with TNT but not with DLC (B1=0.52[0.23-0.79]; B2=0.84[0.44-1.22]). Both FMs reduced oxygen saturation in B1 (0.56 [0.07-1.03] and 0.69 [0.09-1.28]) but only DLC did so in B2 (0.66 [0.11-1.13]). Both end tidal CO
                  <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                  (B1=0.23[0.05-0.4] and 0.71[0.38-1.02]; B2=0.56[0.2-0.9] and 1.20[0.65-1.68]) and mixed-expired-CO
                  <jats:sub>2</jats:sub>
                  (B1=0.74[0.38-1.08] 1.71[1.03-2.37], B2=0.94[0.45-1.38] and 1.78[0.97-2.42]) increased with FMs. Ventilatory adaptations imposed during FM exercising influenced blood-lung gas exchange. Larger ESs were seen with DLC. No adverse changes to human health were observed.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Novelty Bullets</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Facemasks affect the breathing pattern by changing the frequency and amplitude of pulmonary ventilation.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>The augmented ventilatory work increases VO2, VCO2, and RPE and promotes non-concerning drops in SpO2 and CO2 retention.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Increased inspiratory and expiratory pressure can account for the reduction in pulmonary physiological dead space.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Physiological Effects of Exercising at Different Intensities Wearing TNT or Double-layer Cotton Facemasks Compared to Not Wearing a Mask
